pricing.source: OpenAI API pricing, standard short-context rates, 2026-06-24

hardChecks.label: Citation source ids
hardChecks.expectation: Every cited source id exists in the source packet

hardChecks.label: Cost metadata
hardChecks.expectation: estimatedUsd is present when pricing is configured

hardChecks.label: Token metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Input, cached-input, and output token counts are coherent

hardChecks.label: Provider and model metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Provider, model, and prompt version are present

hardChecks.label: Judge input boundary
hardChecks.expectation: Judge prompt omits eval-only object keys and holdout tuning fields

hardChecks.label: Judge claim coverage
hardChecks.expectation: LLM judge returns exactly one judgment for every briefing claim

claimJudgments.claimText: Add a pre-publication accuracy gate that compares generated notes to final QA deltas, because an audit found 14% of generated entries described behavior that changed after final QA and QA notes that PR text becomes unreliable after scope cuts.
claimJudgments.explanation: S1 reports the 14% drift rate and recommends checking final QA deltas before publication. S5 says generated notes should compare against the QA delta log because stale PR text is unreliable after scope cuts.

claimJudgments.claimText: Prioritize review of “commitment-like” statements (e.g., migration warnings, compatibility caveats, admin toggles) since customer success reported escalations when a generated note promised an admin toggle that was removed before release, and QA logs show an admin toggle removal and a late-added migration warning.
claimJudgments.explanation: S2 supports prioritizing migration warnings and compatibility caveats and describes the removed admin-toggle escalation. S5 confirms an admin toggle was removed and a migration warning was added late.

claimJudgments.claimText: Implement a lightweight review queue that highlights changed/removed/sensitive claims instead of requiring manual maintenance of a second changelog, aligning with engineering’s willingness to tag QA deltas but not maintain parallel documentation.
claimJudgments.explanation: S3 directly says engineers can tag final-release deltas if lightweight, will not maintain a second/parallel changelog, and recommends a queue highlighting changed, removed, or sensitive claims.

claimJudgments.claimText: Auto-flag and require explicit human approval for any customer-facing notes touching security, data retention, or access control, since compliance requires an approval record and wants sensitive statements easy to spot before publishing.
claimJudgments.explanation: S4 states that security, data retention, and access-control notes require explicit human approval/approval record and that sensitive statements should be easy to find before publishing. Auto-flagging is a reasonable implementation of that requirement.

claimJudgments.claimText: Update reviewer guidance to focus on drift-prone and high-impact items (changed claims, removed features, sensitive categories, and compatibility warnings) rather than rewriting every note, matching the existing editor checklist expectations.
claimJudgments.explanation: S6 instructs reviewers to inspect generated notes for changed claims, removed features, sensitive categories, and compatibility warnings, and says editors are not expected to rewrite every note manually.

recommendationJudgment.explanation: The recommendation is well grounded in the packet: it combines QA-delta validation from S1/S5, targeted review queues from S3/S6, customer-risk prioritization from S2, and explicit compliance approval from S4. It stays within the evidence and frames auto-diff/flagging as workflow advice rather than as a proven existing system.

notes: Hybrid evaluator output: deterministic hard checks plus structured LLM judge evidence. Manual spot checks are still required before claiming product improvement.
